feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Fiori
feed.subtitle: SAP Fiori Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: How can I navigate from customer line items (F0711) into the specific business partner of F3163?
entry.content:
Hello all,

I am wondering if it is possible to add a link into the "Manage Customer Line Items" app to the "Manage Business Partner Master Data" App  which jumps into the related business partner. In standard there are some links available for the semantic object "Customer" which let you jump into the specific customer directly (f.e. (F0046A) or (F0850A)) but not into the related business partner (F3163) directly out of customer line items app.

I managed to build a Launchpad Descriptor Item in the Fiori Launchpad App Manager which could let you jump into the Business Partner App. But unfortunately it does not jump into the specific Business Partner altough the CustomerID is part of the link. Do I have to maintain specific parameters in the Fiori Launchpad App Manager and if yes, which ones?

 

 

Thanks in advance and best regards!

entry.title: Not able to extend "My Inbox(F0862)" application for Purchase Requisition
entry.content:
Hello SAP Experts,

I’m currently working on extending the standard My Inbox app for Purchase Requisition approvals in S/4HANA 2022. The main requirement is to display a list of approvers in the approval detail screen of the app.

I have followed the steps from these SAP Community blogs:
Fiori My Inbox 2.0 – Extend Approve Purchase Order – S/4HANA 1610
Extend My Inbox 2.0 Purchase Requisition S/4 Hana

What I’ve Done:

  • Extended standard CDS views:
    • C_PurRequisitionFs
    • C_PurRequisitionItemFs
    • Added the required fields and defined @ui annotations like UI.LineItem and UI.Identification
  • Verified that the new fields are available in:
    • OData $metadata
    • Annotations preview (/sap/opu/odata/IWFND/catalogservice;v=2/Annotations(TechnicalName='C_PURREQUISITION_FS_ANNO_MDL',Version='0001')/$value)

Issue:

Although the fields appear correctly in the metadata and annotation files, they are not displayed in the UI of the My Inbox app(Purchase Requisition Approval).

What I’ve Verified:

  • CDS annotations are correctly defined
  • OData metadata is refreshed and active

Question:

Has anyone implemented a similar approver list extension for this scenario successfully?

Any suggestions or shared experiences would be greatly appreciated.
Thank you!

entry.title: F2071 create notification while saving Creating operations are disabled for entity 'EAM_OBJPG_MAINTN
entry.content:
Hi experts,

In F2071 there is a hidden button 'create', we can make it visible via adapt-ui

When we click on 'create' and try to save the notification the following message appears


Creating operations are disabled for entity 'EAM_OBJPG_MAINTNOTIFICATION~C_OBJPGMAINTNOTIFICATI'
Found note 3155352 , but this one is old from 2022.

app details

When we check the /sap/opu/odata/sap/EAM_OBJPG_MAINTNOTIFICATION_SRV/$metadata , and search for 'sap:creatable=',  we see a lot of sap:creatable="false" (336 times)

Does anyone know if it's possible to make it workable? Or is this the reason why the button is hidden?  😁

Thanks in advance

entry.title: Adapt Filters Fiori could not work (No Data)
entry.content:
Dear all,

I have been facing issue that related with Fiori Adapt Filters, especially on Visual Filter,

When I try to use Adapt Filters, it just displays "No Data", How should I do to resolve this issue?
Whereas, The service has been active, and I have tested with SAP Gateway Client the result was 200 Success 

@AbapCatalog.sqlViewName: 'ZSYARD27V_ALP'
@AbapCatalog.compiler.compareFilter: true
@AbapCatalog.preserveKey: true
@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_REQUIRED
@OData.publish: true
@EndUserText.label: 'CDS for ALP'


//Logic for KPI card
@UI.selectionPresentationVariant: [
{
//The below qualifier will be there in my mainfest.json file
qualifier: 'KPIORDDIV',
presentationVariantQualifier: 'KPIORDDIVPV',
selectionVariantQualifier: 'KPIORDDIVSEL'
},

{
qualifier: 'KPIORDDIV1',
presentationVariantQualifier: 'KPIORDDIVPV1',
selectionVariantQualifier: 'KPIORDDIVSEL1'
}
]


//Logic for Default Chart w.r.t Sales Organization
@UI.chart: [
{
qualifier: 'chartDefault',
chartType: #COLUMN,
description: 'Sales Information',
dimensions: ['salesorg'],
measures: ['order_qty_total_val'],  //We have to use the Alias Name as Measure in case if we have given so
dimensionAttributes: [{dimension: 'salesorg', role: #CATEGORY }],
measureAttributes: [{measure: 'order_qty_total_val', role: #AXIS_1 }]
},
//Below logic will be for Chart w.r.t Visual Filter
{
qualifier: 'visualDivision',
chartType: #DONUT,
description: 'Division Description',
dimensions: ['divisiondesc'],
measures: ['order_qty_total_val'],
dimensionAttributes: [{dimension: 'divisiondesc', role: #CATEGORY }],
measureAttributes: [{measure: 'order_qty_total_val', role: #AXIS_1 }]
},

//The below Visual Filter is from KPI perspective and not from Visual Filter Perspective
{
qualifier: 'visualDivision1',
chartType: #LINE,
description: 'Division Description',
dimensions: ['divisiondesc'],
measures: ['open_qty_total_val'],
dimensionAttributes: [{dimension: 'divisiondesc', role: #CATEGORY }],
measureAttributes: [{measure: 'open_qty_total_val', role: #AXIS_1 }]
},

{
qualifier: 'visualDistribution',
chartType: #BAR,
description: 'Distribution Channel Description',
dimensions: ['distchldesc'],
measures: ['order_qty_total_val'],
dimensionAttributes: [{dimension: 'distchldesc', role: #CATEGORY }],
measureAttributes: [{measure: 'order_qty_total_val', role: #AXIS_1 }]
},

{
qualifier: 'VisualPlantDesc',
chartType: #LINE,
description: 'Filter by Plant Description',
dimensions: ['plantdesc'],
measures: ['order_qty_total_val'],
dimensionAttributes: [{ dimension: 'plantdesc', role: #CATEGORY }],
measureAttributes: [{ measure: 'order_qty_total_val', role: #AXIS_2 }]
},

{
qualifier: 'visualMonthYear',
chartType: #LINE,
description: 'Sales by Month-Year',
dimensions: ['document_date'],
measures: ['order_qty_total_val'],
dimensionAttributes: [{dimension: 'document_date', role: #CATEGORY }],
measureAttributes: [{measure: 'order_qty_total_val', role: #AXIS_1 }]
}

]

// Presentationvariant -> This is required to generate the UI layout; Without which we wont be able to see
//   the Default Chart
@UI.presentationVariant: [
{
//The qualifier name inside Visualizations have to be same as that give for @ui.chart...and the other main qualifier
//  has to be maintained in the manifest.json file under Settings under pages
qualifier: 'Default', visualizations: [{type: #AS_CHART, qualifier: 'chartDefault' }]
},
//Here we will start adding the Visual Filter Chart Qualifier as in the Presentation Variant Qualifier
{
qualifier: 'PresentationVisualDiv', visualizations: [{type: #AS_CHART, qualifier: 'visualDivision' }]
},

{
qualifier: 'presentationVisualDist', visualizations: [{type: #AS_CHART, qualifier: 'visualDistribution' }]
},

 {
 qualifier: 'PresentationVisualPlnt', visualizations: [{type: #AS_CHART, qualifier: 'VisualPlantDesc' }]
 },

{
qualifier: 'presentationMonthYear', sortOrder: [{ by: 'document_date',direction: #ASC }],
visualizations: [{type: #AS_CHART, qualifier: 'visualMonthYear' }]
},

//The below logic is for KPI card
{
qualifier: 'KPIORDDIVPV',
//The below text is optional as I didn't find any impact in UI
//text: 'KPI: Order Qty by Division',
//The KPI chart w.r.t Dimension will be same as that of Visual Filter
visualizations: [{type: #AS_CHART, qualifier: 'visualDivision' },
{type: #AS_DATAPOINT, qualifier: 'order_qty_total_val' }]
},

//Logic for 2nd KPI card
{
qualifier: 'KPIORDDIVPV1',
visualizations: [{type: #AS_CHART, qualifier: 'visualDivision1' },
{type: #AS_DATAPOINT, qualifier: 'open_qty_total_val' }]
}
]

@UI.selectionVariant: [{
qualifier: 'KPIORDDIVSEL'

}]

@UI.headerInfo: {typeName: 'Sales Report', typeNamePlural: 'Sales Report'}
define view ZCDS_ALP_REPORT as select from ZSALESDATA_HEAD
left outer join t247 as month on  month.spras = $session.system_language
                                  and month.mnr   = substring(documentdate, 5, 2)
{

@UI.lineItem: [{position: 10 }]
@EndUserText.label: 'Sales Document'
key salesdoc,
@UI.lineItem: [{position: 20 }]
key item@<EndUserText.label: 'Item',
key salesorg,
@UI.selectionField: [{position: 10 }]
@UI.lineItem: [{position: 30 }]
@EndUserText.label: 'Plant'
plant,
@UI.lineItem: [{position: 40 }]
@EndUserText.label: 'Plant Description'
plantdesc@<UI.selectionField: [{ position: 20 }],
@UI.selectionField: [{position: 40 }]  //Mandatory for Visual filter and changing the position changes the Visual Filters layput
@UI.lineItem: [{position: 50 }]
@EndUserText.label: 'Division Description'
divisiondesc,
@UI.selectionField: [{position: 30 }]
@UI.lineItem: [{position: 60 }]
@EndUserText.label: 'Distribution Channel Description'
distchldesc,
//The text that we specify as a part of Title; this will get displayed on my KPI..
@UI.dataPoint.title: 'Total Order Quantity Value'
@UI.lineItem: [{position: 70 }]
@EndUserText.label: 'Order Quantity Total Value'
@DefaultAggregation: #SUM
orderqtytotalvalue as order_qty_total_val,
//We will use the document_date alias for showing the output w.r.t Sorted Order (Ascending)
//This annotation renders the Text Element to the UI
@ObjectModel.text.element: ['month_year']
//The below annotation renders only the Text to the UI front-end and the value #TEXT_ONLY is the best option
@UI.textArrangement: #TEXT_ONLY
substring(documentdate, 1, 6) as document_date@<UI.lineItem: [{ position: 80 }] @<UI.selectionField: [{ position: 50 }] @<EndUserText.label: 'Document Date',
//Date Formatting eg:- JAN-2020
concat(concat(month.ktx,'-'),substring(documentdate, 1, 4)) as
month_year,
@DefaultAggregation: #SUM
openqtytotalvalue as open_qty_total_val@<UI.dataPoint.title: 'Total Open Quantity Value' @<EndUserText.label: 'Open Quantity Total Value'
}
{
  "_version": "1.48.0",
  "sap.app": {
    "id": "fiorianalytics",
    "type": "application",
    "i18n": "i18n/i18n.properties",
    "applicationVersion": {
      "version": "0.0.1"
    },
    "title": "{{appTitle}}",
    "description": "{{appDescription}}",
    "resources": "resources.json",
    "sourceTemplate": {
      "id": "@sap/generator-fiori:alp",
      "version": "1.17.3",
      "toolsId": "85af8d2e-e6e7-4ec1-8e23-643077416d97"
    },
    "dataSources": {
      "ZCDS_ALP_REPORT_CDS_VAN": {
        "uri": "/sap/opu/odata/IWFND/CATALOGSERVICE;v=2/Annotations(TechnicalName='ZCDS_ALP_REPORT_CDS_VAN',Version='0001')/$value/",
        "type": "ODataAnnotation",
        "settings": {
          "localUri": "localService/mainService/ZCDS_ALP_REPORT_CDS_VAN.xml"
        }
      },
      "annotation": {
        "type": "ODataAnnotation",
        "uri": "annotations/annotation.xml",
        "settings": {
          "localUri": "annotations/annotation.xml"
        }
      },
      "mainService": {
        "uri": "/sap/opu/odata/sap/ZCDS_ALP_REPORT_CDS/",
        "type": "OData",
        "settings": {
          "annotations": [
            "ZCDS_ALP_REPORT_CDS_VAN",
            "annotation"
          ],
          "localUri": "localService/mainService/metadata.xml",
          "odataVersion": "2.0"
        }
      }
    }
  },
  "sap.ui": {
    "technology": "UI5",
    "icons": {
      "icon": "",
      "favIcon": "",
      "phone": "",
      "phone@2": "",
      "tablet": "",
      "tablet@2": ""
    },
    "deviceTypes": {
      "desktop": true,
      "tablet": true,
      "phone": true
    }
  },
  "sap.ui5": {
    "flexEnabled": true,
    "dependencies": {
      "minUI5Version": "1.108.9",
      "libs": {
        "sap.m": {},
        "sap.ui.core": {},
        "sap.ushell": {},
        "sap.f": {},
        "sap.ui.comp": {},
        "sap.ui.generic.app": {},
        "sap.suite.ui.generic.template": {}
      }
    },
    "contentDensities": {
      "compact": true,
      "cozy": true
    },
    "models": {
      "i18n": {
        "type": "sap.ui.model.resource.ResourceModel",
        "settings": {
          "bundleName": "fiorianalytics.i18n.i18n"
        }
      },
      "": {
        "dataSource": "mainService",
        "preload": true,
        "settings": {
          "defaultBindingMode": "TwoWay",
          "defaultCountMode": "Inline",
          "refreshAfterChange": false,
          "metadataUrlParams": {
            "sap-value-list": "none"
          }
        }
      },
      "@i18n": {
        "type": "sap.ui.model.resource.ResourceModel",
        "uri": "i18n/i18n.properties"
      }
    },
    "resources": {
      "css": []
    },
    "routing": {
      "config": {},
      "routes": [],
      "targets": {}
    }
  },
  "sap.ui.generic.app": {
    "_version": "1.3.0",
    "settings": {
      "forceGlobalRefresh": false,
      "objectPageHeaderType": "Dynamic",
      "considerAnalyticalParameters": true,
      "showDraftToggle": false
    },
    "pages": {
      "AnalyticalListPage|ZCDS_ALP_REPORT": {
        "entitySet": "ZCDS_ALP_REPORT",
        "component": {
          "name": "sap.suite.ui.generic.template.AnalyticalListPage",
          "list": true,
          "settings": {
            "condensedTableLayout": true,
            "showGoButtonOnFilterBar": true,
            "useBatch": false,
            "autoHide": true,
            "qualifier": "Default",
            "smartVariantManagement": false,
            "selectionPresentationVariant": {
        "selectionVariant": "KPIORDDIVSEL",
        "presentationVariant": "KPIORDDIVPV"
      },
            "tableSettings": {
              "multiSelect": false,
              "type": "AnalyticalTable"
            },
            "keyPerformanceIndicators": {}
          }
        },
        "pages": {
          "ObjectPage|ZCDS_ALP_REPORT": {
            "entitySet": "ZCDS_ALP_REPORT",
            "defaultLayoutTypeIfExternalNavigation": "MidColumnFullScreen",
            "component": {
              "name": "sap.suite.ui.generic.template.ObjectPage"
            }
          }
        }
      }
    }
  },
  "sap.fiori": {
    "registrationIds": [],
    "archeType": "analytical"
  }
}



Thank You,
Best Regards.

DafinTempur77

entry.title: Can I add an annotation to a standard SAP CDS view if it does not affect compatibility?
entry.content:
I am working on a task to add a new field and hide existing fields from an "Assigned to" search help in Fiori App F5104A (Perform Maintenance Jobs). I have extended the "Assigned To" search help view to add a new field for division. In addition, in order to hide existing fields, I need to add a metadata extension. However, I get an error in the metadata extension shown below.  When I attempt to add the annotation to the standard base view, I get the warnings shown below as well. According to my findings it seems that if I do not modify or remove any existing parameters, associations, name changes, then anything else should not affect incompatibility. Is it ok to add the annotation "@Metadata.allowExtensions: true" to the standard base view in order to proceed with the metadata extension? The standard CDS view that has been extended and annotated is I_PersWrkAgrmtSrchHelp. 

"Assigned To" field in Fiori App F5104A:

"Assigned To" search help with division added:

 

Error in metadata extension:

 

Warning in standard CDS view when attempting to add "@Metadata.allowExtensions: true".

4

Link to the release contract (Use System-Internally (C1)) mentioned in the above warning:

Release Contracts | ABAP Keyword Documentation

I would appreciate the feedback in order to help my team and I move forward with this.

 

Thank you!

Patrick

entry.title: Workflow approval rebate and commission
entry.content:
Dear Experts

Is there a workflow approval for sales rebate and commission in SAP S/4HANA RISE?

entry.title: HTML5 APP run failed in BTP Trial Account
entry.content:
Dear Expert,

base on Tutorial : Generate an SAP Fiori Elements Application based on a RAP-based Service

i created the odata service successfully and published.

i import this odata into BAS , preview was ok. then i deployed it to HTML5 APP. 

but when in ran this app, nothing showed and found below error in F12, could you guide me how to resolve it .

 

 

Thanks!!

entry.title: the Fiori app “Change Inbound Deliveries – Deliveries expandability
entry.content:
Hello everyone,
I have received the requirement to extend the Fiori app “Change Inbound Deliveries – Deliveries” by adding a new custom field. My initial plan was to add this field to the ABAP structure, expose it through the relevant OData entity, and finally enhance the Fiori app using the SAP Business Application Studio.

However, while reviewing the official SAP documentation, I noticed that this app is marked as not extensible.

Could you please let me know whether it is still possible to enhance this standard Fiori app with a custom field? If so, what would be the recommended approach?

Thank you very much in advance for your support.
Jalal

entry.title: Issue with file uploader in sap Fiori custom apps after upgrade
entry.content:
Hi Experts, 

Recently we have upgraded our HANA version from 2020 to 2023 and also the UI5 version from 1.84 to 1.120.

File uploader control is not working as expecting in custom fiori application after upgrade , getting the error "Cannot read properties of undefined (reading '0')" while reading file[0] and below is the code .

Above same code is working fine in non upgraded system. Can some suggest how to fix this issue ? 

KR,

Anji

entry.title: Fiori Elements + RAP: How to preserve order of batch action calls? Wrong Batch order.
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I'm working on a Fiori Elements app based on RAP (ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model).
The user scans equipment one by one and then triggers a Print Label action.
The labels should be printed in the exact order the equipment was scanned, but sometimes they are printed in the wrong order.

What I observed:

  • The action printLabel is defined in the behavior definition.

  • When multiple rows are selected and the action is triggered, the backend method is called multiple times — once for each entity.

  • Although the UI sends a $batch, the printLabel method is executed separately per entity, and not necessarily in order.

This results in labels being printed in the wrong sequence, which causes confusion and extra manual effort.

My Behavior definition:

managed implementation in class ZBP_I_Equipment
strict(2);

define behavior for Z_I_Equipment alias Equipment
persistent table I_Equipment
lock master
{
    field( readonly ) Equipment;
    action printLabel;
}

 

My questions:

  1. Is there a way to ensure that printLabel is called once with all selected entries, preserving their original selection order?

  2. Is it possible to change the behavior to support multiple processing (e.g. on entity ... multiple) in this RAP setup?

  3. Are there best practices for preserving processing order in such scenarios?

Any guidance or working examples would be greatly appreciated.
Thank you!

entry.title: Fiori APP / Odata V2 to API
entry.content:
I have created a FIORI App with classical odata V2.

I'd like to use API data with Odata by using CPI

Instead of 
APP (odata) -> destination back-end 
APP (OData) -> Destination CPI -> Use API

I suppose this is possible but I don't know exactly how to do that.

I have 2 sub-account. One for the APP, One for Integration Suite. 

Do you know steps to acheive that ? Ideally with an example.

Thanks gurus for your pov on this little challenge

entry.title: SAP FIORI - Upload General Journal Entries
entry.content:
Hello everyone, 

When using the Fiori App "Upload General Journal Entries" to upload general journal entries via Excel, I receive the following error message: 

UI5-Komponente for navigation view "#FinanceApplicationLog-showList?DateFrom=&DateTo=&LogExternalId=Header&LogObjectId=FIGL_UPLDGLJE&LogObjectSubId=&PersKey=fin.gl.journalentry.upload.ShowLog&sap-app-origin-hint=&sap-xapp-state=TAS21Q6XL0RC1NE8F29FAEWYBAI18Kxxxxxxxxx" could not be loaded. 

Did anyone face a similar situation or found a solution?

 

Best 

Martin

entry.title: End-to-End Maintenance Execution Using SAP Fiori Apps in SAP S/4HANA – A Phase-Wise Approach
entry.content:
Introduction:
In modern asset-intensive industries, the reliability and availability of technical assets directly impact business performance. SAP Plant Maintenance (SAP PM) in S/4HANA, integrated with SAP Fiori, transforms the traditional maintenance execution process into a user-centric, phase-wise digital journey.

This blog outlines the End-to-End Maintenance Execution Cycle using standard SAP Fiori apps, structured by maintenance phases – from initiation to completion.

Maintenance Execution Phases & SAP Fiori Apps:

Below is a breakdown of the 9 maintenance phases, each mapped with relevant SAP Fiori applications used in SAP S/4HANA:

1. Initiation Phase – Raise Maintenance Request

  • App:

    • Create Maintenance Request (F1511A)
      https://fioriappslibrary.hana.ondemand.com/sap/fix/externalViewer/#/detail/Apps('F1511A')/S30PCE

      Fiori ID: F1511A

    • My Maintenance Requests (F4513)
      https://fioriappslibrary.hana.ondemand.com/sap/fix/externalViewer/#/detail/Apps('F4513')/S30PCE

      Fiori ID: F4513

  • Business Role: Technician / Operator

  • Purpose: Report asset breakdown, malfunction, or maintenance need.

2. Screening Phase – Request Evaluation

  • App:

    • Screen Maintenance Request (F4072)
      https://fioriappslibrary.hana.ondemand.com/sap/fix/externalViewer/#/detail/Apps('F4072')/S30PCE

      Fiori ID: F4072 

  • Business Role: Maintenance Supervisor

  • Purpose: Validate, prioritize, and convert valid requests into maintenance notifications.

3. Planning Phase – Notification Planning

  • App:

    • Find Maintenance Notification (F2071)
      https://fioriappslibrary.hana.ondemand.com/sap/fix/externalViewer/#/detail/Apps('F2071')/S30PCE

      Fiori ID: F2071

  • Business Role: Maintenance Planner

  • Purpose: Plan operations, materials, required components, work centers, and document scope.

4. Approval Phase – Workflow Approval

  • App:

    • My Inbox (F0862)
      https://fioriappslibrary.hana.ondemand.com/sap/fix/externalViewer/#/detail/Apps('F0862')/S26OP

      Fiori ID: F0862

       

       

  • Business Role: Department Manager / Approver

  • Purpose: Approve maintenance plans based on cost, scope, and operational needs.

5. Preparation Phase – Capacity Planning

  • Apps:

    • Manage Maintenance Planning Buckets (F3888)
      https://fioriappslibrary.hana.ondemand.com/sap/fix/externalViewer/#/detail/Apps('F3888')/S30PCE

      Fiori ID: F3888

    • Manage Maintenance Buckets (F4073)
      https://fioriappslibrary.hana.ondemand.com/sap/fix/externalViewer/#/detail/Apps('F4073')/S30PCE 

      Fiori ID: F4073

       

  • Business Role: Maintenance Planner

  • Purpose: Review capacity, allocate resources into planning buckets.

6. Scheduling Phase – Order Scheduling

  • App:

    • Find Maintenance Order (F2175)
      https://fioriappslibrary.hana.ondemand.com/sap/fix/externalViewer/#/detail/Apps('F2175')/S30PCE

      Fiori ID: F2175

       

  • Business Role: Scheduler

  • Purpose: Schedule maintenance orders against available capacity and resource windows.

7. Execution Phase – Perform Maintenance

  • App:

    • Perform Maintenance Jobs (F5104A)
      https://fioriappslibrary.hana.ondemand.com/sap/fix/externalViewer/#/detail/Apps('F5104A')/S30PCE

      Fiori ID: F5104A

       

  • Business Role: Maintenance Technician

  • Purpose: Execute job cards, record time confirmations, consumption, and failure causes.

8. Post-Execution Phase – Technical Completion

  • App:

    • Find Maintenance Order (Set to TECO)
      https://fioriappslibrary.hana.ondemand.com/sap/fix/externalViewer/#/detail/Apps('F2175')/S30PCE

      Fiori ID: F2175

       

  • Business Role: Maintenance Supervisor

  • Purpose: Confirm job completion, close technical operations.

9. Completion Phase – Business Closure

  • App:

    • Find Maintenance Order (Set to CLSD)
      https://fioriappslibrary.hana.ondemand.com/sap/fix/externalViewer/#/detail/Apps('F2175')/S30PCE

      Fiori ID: F2175

       

  • Business Role: Maintenance Planner / Controller

  • Purpose: Final financial settlement, order closure, cost analysis.


Business Benefits of Fiori-Based Maintenance Phases:

  • Role-based access & intuitive UI

  • Improved maintenance planning & execution accuracy

  • Faster approvals via integrated workflow

  • Real-time visibility into asset status

  • Seamless collaboration between maintenance teams

Conclusion:
SAP S/4HANA with Fiori delivers an intelligent, mobile-ready, and user-friendly maintenance experience. By aligning the maintenance lifecycle with structured Fiori apps, organizations can reduce asset downtime, streamline approvals, and achieve operational excellence.

entry.title: I am currently working on a custom Ui5 app, where i use third part software to access documents
entry.content:
My custom Ui app is using Alfresco server to fetch documents, I make the call via a API portal, which is accessed via a BTP destination.

 

it works well, but it needs a x-api-key in the http header, when i make a request call.

I am looking for best solution to pass this x-api-key in the request, I want to hardcode it in the code, 

I have tried adding this to additional parameters of the destination, it does not work, 

I have passed encrypted key as well, but it also returns unauthorized.

Any idea on how can i pass this x-api-key securely to my http request via BTP destination?

 

any use case for SAP credentials store foe such requirement?

SAP Fiori Launchpad @ui5_dev SAP BTP Security development tools for SAP BTP, Neo environment JavaScript SAP Fiori for SAP ERP

entry.title: Restrict Raw Material Batch transfer into storage location
entry.content:
Hi,

I would like to restrict transfers into a storage location for raw material transfer because it is using for HALB materials.

Is it possible?

Thank you.

entry.title: WDY_CONF_USER Config_ID can the entries in this Table be overwritten
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

We have created some Fiori apps and have translations done. We now want/Need to transport the table WDY_CONF_USER along with the apps but we can not see if the Config Id is one of a kind or if this number could be in another system and be overwritten as it is the same, when we transport this table.

 

Kind regards

Mark Wright

entry.title: Set Default Filter Value for Work Center Category in Create Maintenance Request (F1511A) App
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

We are using the standard SAP Fiori App "Create Maintenance Request" (F1511A). When user clicks on the value help for the Main Work Center, a dialog opens where they can search using filters such as Work Center, Plant and Work Center Category. 

As part of business requirements, they would like the Work Center Category filter to default to 0005 (Plant Maintenance) each time the value help dialog is opened.

We explored UI adaptation options using SAP Business Application Studio. Checked for controller extensions. 

Is there any supported way to default the Work Center Category filter field to 0005 in the value help dialog for Main Work Center. If yes, kindly advise how we can achieve this.

entry.title: How to add Odata Service to a Fiori App?
entry.content:
I have created an OData service in Eclipse(It is not present in /IWFND/MAINT_SERVICE) and I was trying to connect it to my Fiori App in VS Code. However, I encountered the following error:

VS Code error:

Browser error when trying to access the HTTPS URL:

entry.title: Issue with deleted Purchase Requisition still generating MRP demand (Fiori App F4795)
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I am facing a situation related to purchase requisitions created by MRP and deleted via the Fiori App F4795 — Hand Over Purchase Requisitions (F4795).

Scenario:

A purchase requisition generated by MRP for the parent item was deleted via the Fiori App F4795 Hand Over Purchase Requisitions (Table EBAN, field LOEKZ = X).

However, the MRP still shows a Subassembly Requirement (BB) for the child item, with the same quantity and date as the deleted requisition.

The parent component (semi-finished product) still has a reservation that was not cleared (Table RESB, field KZEAR ≠ X).

  • Has anyone faced similar behavior, where a deleted requisition still generates MRP demand due to an open Subassembly Requirement or an uncleared reservation?
  • Is there any official solution or correction note for this?
  • What is the best practice to ensure that deleted requisitions do not continue generating demand in MRP when using F4795?

I have read the article by @Caetano about this app and would appreciate any help with this difficulty.

Thank you very much in advance for your support!

entry.title: How do I consume/display the data of Odata on Fiori App?
entry.content:
How do I consume/display the OData on my Fiori App? I've run my fiori app in local

Result in Gateway Client in SAP GUI:

 

Result in Fiori App:

I was expected sales document number instead of Chang_121 and user instead of Changed_121.
